title: Viking Therapeutics (VKTX) Q3 2024 Earnings Summary: Strong Cash Position and Pipeline Progress
text: Viking Therapeutics reported a net loss of $24.9 million for Q3 2024, compared to $22.5 million in the prior year. The company's research and development expenses increased to $22.8 million from $18.4 million year-over-year, while general and administrative costs rose to $13.8 million from $8.9 million. For the nine-month period, the biotech firm posted a net loss of $74.5 million, with R&D expenses reaching $70.7 million. Viking strengthened its balance sheet significantly, holding $930 million in cash, cash equivalents, and short-term investments as of September 30, 2024—a substantial increase from $362 million at year-end 2023. The Phase II VENTURE trial of VK2735 demonstrated weight reductions of up to 14.7% from baseline, positioning this as the company's primary asset. The Phase IIb VOYAGE study of VK2809 met its primary endpoints for MASH treatment, showing improvements in disease resolution and fibrosis markers. Management indicated the subcutaneous formulation represents an 80% opportunity, with oral dosing as a secondary commercial opportunity. The robust cash position provides runway for advancing Phase III development of VK2735 and continuing studies across the pipeline.
